Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No.: W-03-189
Dear Assemblymember Leslie:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice on Government Code
$ 1090 and its application to a potential conflict of interest created by the appointrnent of
Alan Clarke to the Cameron Park Community Services District Board. Under the
provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8XD), copy enclosed, we must decline yogr request for
advice. Govemment Code $ 1090 is outside the purvidw of the Fair Political practices
Commission - we advise on laws only pertaining to the Political Reform Act.l
Please direct your inquiry to the Office of the Attorney General. Also, we are not
authorized to provide written advice with respect to third parties. (Section 831 14;
regulation 18329.)
Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 327-8691 if you have any questions.
Sincerely,
Re:

sections.conclusion: Under the provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the FPPC must decline the request for advice. Government Code § 1090 is outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission - the FPPC advises on laws only pertaining to the Political Reform Act. The inquiry should be directed to the Office of the Attorney General, and the FPPC is not authorized to provide written advice with respect to third parties.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Government Code § 1090 creates a conflict of interest regarding the appointment of Alan Clarke to the Cameron Park Community Services District Board.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because Government Code § 1090 falls outside its jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act, and the FPPC cannot provide written advice regarding third parties. The requesting party should contact the Office of the Attorney General instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear explanation of jurisdictional limits. No formal Question/Conclusion sections with headers, but substantive content is present in body text. Reference to regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) provides regulatory basis for declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Government Code § 1090 creates a conflict of interest regarding the appointment of Alan Clarke to the Cameron Park Community Services District Board.

CONCLUSION: Under the provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the FPPC must decline the request for advice. Government Code § 1090 is outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission - the FPPC advises on laws only pertaining to the Political Reform Act. The inquiry should be directed to the Office of the Attorney General, and the FPPC is not authorized to provide written advice with respect to third parties.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines Assemblymember Tim Leslie's request for written advice on a potential conflict of interest under Government Code § 1090 regarding an appointment to the Cameron Park Community Services District Board, as that statute is outside the Commission's purview.
